title: dorycnium pentaphyllum var. dumulosum
intro: Dorycnium pentaphyllum var. dumulosum, also known as the prickly clover or Jerusalem clover, is a perennial plant species in the legume family Fabaceae, characterized by its prickly stems and clusters of small white flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating various health issues. The health benefits associated with Dorycnium pentaphyllum var. dumulosum include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and soothe skin irritations. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
